You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 87153ae1).

## Issue to Resolve

Formatting enum class with member definition and missing trailing commas causes rule violations on first run
## Expected Behavior
<!---Tell us what should happen. -->
<!-- If relevant, provide a link to the relevant section in the [Kotlin Coding Conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) or [Android Kotlin Style Guide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide).  -->

<!-- Note: Ktlint should not produce any code which conflict with IntelliJ default code formatting. So please verify that your expectation is accepted by IntelliJ default code formatting. -->

When formatting and validating the following enum class, the ktlint command should exit successfully (status `0`):

```kotlin
enum class TestEnum() {
    A,
    B;

    companion object {
        const val A_NUMBER = 1L
    }
}
```

## Observed Behavior
<!---Tell us what happens instead of the expected behavior -->
<!--- Provide the exact command which was executed but please -->
<!--- ensure that the flag "--log-level=debug" is added to the -->
<!--- command as well. Provide the output of this command. -->

When running ktlint on the above enum class, it is formatted correctly, but the run fails with rule violations (exit status `1`):
```bash
$ ktlint --format 
17:25:00.694 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
/home/karl/tasks/ktlint-issue-example/TestEnum.kt:1:1: Missing trailing comma before "}" (trailing-comma-on-declaration-site)
/home/karl/tasks/ktlint-issue-example/TestEnum.kt:5:4: Unnecessary semicolon (no-semi)

Summary error count (descending) by rule:
  no-semi: 1
  trailing-comma-on-declaration-site: 1
Exit status: 1 
```

I note that the line number for the `no-semi` violation is the line number after formatting.

A second run on the (now properly formatted) code runs correctly (exit status `0`):
```bash
$ ktlint --format
17:25:05.397 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
Exit status: 0
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Run command on above enum class:
```ktlint --format```

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.1
* Operating System and version: Arch Linux

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I have the same issue

See release notes of 0.48.0:
> * The default value for trailing comma's on call site is changed to `true` unless the `android codestyle` is enabled. Note that KtLint from a consistency viewpoint *enforces* the trailing comma on call site while default IntelliJ IDEA formatting only *allows* the trailing comma but leaves it up to the developer's discretion. ([#1670](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1670))

@paul-dingemans I don't quite follow. My issue is that after doing formatting, ktlint reports invalid  `no-semi` and `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` rule violations. A subsequent run of ktlint (correctly) passes the same code.

Ah now I see. Although it is not visual in the textual representation of the code, the trailing comma is not inserted at the correct place in the AST representation of the code. I will try to fix it.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `RELEASING.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

Let me analyze this issue step by step:

1. The problem is that when formatting an enum class with member definitions and missing trailing commas, ktlint reports rule violations on the first run but passes on the second run.

2. The issue mentions that the trailing comma is not being inserted at the correct place in the AST representation of the code.

3. The relevant file is `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`

Let me start by reading the relevant files to understand the codebase and the issue.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.ARROW
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.CLASS
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.FUNCTION_TYPE
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.WHEN_ENTRY
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.children
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.isCodeLeaf
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.lineIndent
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.noNewLineInClosedRange
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.prevCodeLeaf
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.prevLeaf
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 24→import kotlin.properties.Delegates
 25→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 26→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValueParser
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 28→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiElement
 29→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 30→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.TokenSet
 31→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtClass
 32→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtCollectionLiteralExpression
 33→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtDestructuringDeclaration
 34→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtEnumEntry
 35→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtFunctionLiteral
 36→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtParameterList
 37→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtPsiFactory
 38→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtValueArgumentList
 39→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtWhenEntry
 40→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtWhenExpression
 41→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.anyDescendantOfType
 42→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.collectDescendantsOfType
 43→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.nextLeaf
 44→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.parents
 45→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.prevLeaf
 46→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.cast
 47→
 48→/**
 49→ * Linting trailing comma for declaration site.
 50→ *
 51→ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas)
 52→ */
 53→public class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule :
 54→    Rule(
 55→        id = "trailing-comma-on-declaration-site",
 56→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 57→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 58→                ruleId = "standard:wrapping",
 59→                loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded = true,
 60→                runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled = true,
 61→            ),
 62→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 63→        ),
 64→    ),
 65→    UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 66→
 67→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = listOf(
 68→        TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY,
 69→    )
 70→
 71→    private var allowTrailingComma by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 72→
 73→    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() =
 74→        elementType in TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE && allowTrailingComma
 75→
 76→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
 77→        allowTrailingComma = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY)
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 81→        node: ASTNode,
 82→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→    ) {
 85→        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 86→        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 87→        when (node.elementType) {
 88→            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 92→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 93→            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94→            else -> Unit
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(
 99→        node: ASTNode,
100→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
101→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
102→    ) {
103→        val inspectNode = node
104→            .children()
105→            .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
106→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
107→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
108→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
109→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
110→            emit = emit,
111→        )
112→    }
113→
114→    private fun visitFunctionLiteral(
115→        node: ASTNode,
116→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
118→    ) {
119→        val inspectNode = node
120→            .children()
121→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ARROW }
122→            ?: // lambda w/o an arrow -> no arguments -> no commas
123→            return
124→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
125→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
126→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
127→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
128→            emit = emit,
129→        )
130→    }
131→
132→    private fun visitValueList(
133→        node: ASTNode,
134→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
135→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
136→    ) {
137→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
138→            node
139→                .children()
140→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
141→                ?.let { inspectNode ->
142→                    node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
143→                        inspectNode = inspectNode,
144→                        isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
145→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
146→                        emit = emit,
147→                    )
148→                }
149→        }
150→    }
151→
152→    private fun visitTypeList(
153→        node: ASTNode,
154→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
156→    ) {
157→        val inspectNode = node
158→            .children()
159→            .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
160→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
161→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
162→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
163→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
164→            emit = emit,
165→        )
166→    }
167→
168→    private fun visitWhenEntry(
169→        node: ASTNode,
170→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
171→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
172→    ) {
173→        val psi = node.psi
174→        require(psi is KtWhenEntry)
175→        if (psi.isElse || psi.parent.cast<KtWhenExpression>().leftParenthesis == null) {
176→            // no commas for "else" or when there are no opening parenthesis for the when-expression
177→            return
178→        }
179→
180→        val inspectNode = node
181→            .children()
182→            .first { it.elementType == ARROW }
183→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
184→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
185→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
186→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
187→            emit = emit,
188→        )
189→    }
190→    private fun visitClass(
191→        node: ASTNode,
192→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
193→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
194→    ) {
195→        val psi = node.psi
196→        require(psi is KtClass)
197→
198→        node
199→            .takeIf { psi.isEnum() }
200→            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)
201→            ?.takeUnless {
202→                // Do nothing when last two entries are on same line as no trailing comma should be inserted
203→                it.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()
204→            }?.let { classBody ->
205→                val nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition = classBody.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()
206→                node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
207→                    inspectNode = nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition,
208→                    isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
209→                    autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
210→                    emit = emit,
211→                )
212→            }
213→    }
214→
215→    /**
216→     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
217→     *
218→     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
219→     * last element of the class.
220→     */
221→    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition(): ASTNode {
222→        val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
223→
224→        val semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry = lastEnumEntry
225→            .children()
226→            .singleOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }
227→
228→        return semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry ?: lastChildNode
229→    }
230→
231→    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
232→        val lastTwoEnumEntries =
233→            children()
234→                .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
235→                .toList()
236→                .takeLast(2)
237→
238→        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
239→    }
240→
241→    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
242→        inspectNode: ASTNode,
243→        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
244→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
246→    ) {
247→        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
248→        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
249→        val trailingCommaState = when {
250→            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
251→            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
252→        }
253→        when (trailingCommaState) {
254→            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
255→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
256→                    inspectNode
257→                        .treeParent
258→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
259→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
260→                        ?.prevLeaf()
261→                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
262→                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
263→                                emit(
264→                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
265→                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
266→                                    true,
267→                                )
268→                                if (autoCorrect) {
269→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
270→                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
271→                                }
272→                            }
273→                        }
274→                } else {
275→                    emit(
276→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
277→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
278→                        true,
279→                    )
280→                    if (autoCorrect) {
281→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
282→                    }
283→                }
284→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
285→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
286→                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
287→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
288→                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
289→                        emit(
290→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
291→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
292→                            true,
293→                        )
294→                    } else {
295→                        emit(
296→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
297→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
298→                            true,
299→                        )
300→                    }
301→                    if (autoCorrect) {
302→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
303→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
304→                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
305→                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
306→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
307→                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
308→                            } else {
309→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
310→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
311→                            }
312→                        }
313→
314→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
316→                                val parentIndent =
317→                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
318→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321→                                enumEntry.apply {
322→                                    add(newline)
323→                                    removeChild(inspectNode)
324→                                    parent.addAfter(createSemicolon(), this)
325→                                }
326→                            }
327→                        }
328→
329→                        val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
330→                        prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
331→                    }
332→                }
333→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
334→                emit(
335→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
336→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
337→                    true,
338→                )
339→                if (autoCorrect) {
340→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
341→                }
342→            }
343→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
344→        }
345→    }
346→
347→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
348→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
349→        parents()
350→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
351→            .lineIndent()
352→
353→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean = when {
354→        element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> isMultiline(element.parent)
355→        element is KtFunctionLiteral -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
356→        element is KtWhenEntry -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
357→        element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
358→        element is KtValueArgumentList && element.children.size == 1 && element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
359→            // special handling for collection literal
360→            // @Annotation([
361→            //    "something",
362→            // ])
363→            val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
364→            containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
365→        }
366→        element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> false
367→        else -> element.textContains('\n')
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
371→        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
372→            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
373→            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
374→        } else {
375→            false
376→        }
377→
378→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
379→        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
380→            this
381→        } else {
382→            prevCodeLeaf()
383→        }
384→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
385→    }
386→
387→    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(from: PsiElement, to: PsiElement): Boolean {
388→        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
389→        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
390→            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
391→                return true
392→            }
393→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
394→        }
395→        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
396→    }
397→
398→    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
399→        /**
400→         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
401→         */
402→        EXISTS,
403→
404→        /**
405→         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
406→         */
407→        MISSING,
408→
409→        /**
410→         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
411→         */
412→        NOT_EXISTS,
413→
414→        /**
415→         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
416→         */
417→        REDUNDANT,
418→    }
419→
420→    public companion object {
421→        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
422→
423→        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
424→            EditorConfigProperty(
425→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
426→                    "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma",
427→                    "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the defining " +
428→                        "side, e.g. parameter-list, type-argument-list, lambda-value-parameters, enum-entries, etc." +
429→                        "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
430→                        "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
431→                        "trailing comma when set.",
432→                    PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
433→                    BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
434→                ),
435→                defaultValue = true,
436→                defaultAndroidValue = false,
437→            )
438→
439→        @Deprecated(
440→            message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49",
441→            replaceWith = ReplaceWith("TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY"),
442→        )
443→        @Suppress("ktlint:experimental:property-naming")
444→        public val allowTrailingCommaProperty: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
445→            TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY
446→
447→        private val TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE = TokenSet.create(
448→            CLASS,
449→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION,
450→            FUNCTION_LITERAL,
451→            FUNCTION_TYPE,
452→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST,
453→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST,
454→            WHEN_ENTRY,
455→        )
456→    }
457→}

conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
  6→
  7→### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
  8→
  9→Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 10→
 11→API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
 12→
 13→Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
 14→
 15→The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
 16→```editorconfig
 17→[*.{kt,kts}]
 18→ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
 19→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
 20→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
 21→```
 22→Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
 23→
 24→Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
 25→
 26→From the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
 27→```
 28→KtLintRuleEngine(
 29→    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
 30→    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(
 31→        EditorConfig
 32→            .builder()
 33→            .section(
 34→                Section
 35→                    .builder()
 36→                    .glob(Glob("*.{kt,kts}"))
 37→                    .properties(
 38→                        Property
 39→                            .builder()
 40→                            .name("ktlint_experimental")
 41→                            .value("enabled"),
 42→                    ),
 43→            )
 44→            .build()
 45→    )
 46→)
 47→```
 48→If the user has set property `ktlint_experimental` explicitly than that value will be used. If the value is not defined, the value provided via `editorConfigDefaults` will be used.
 49→
 50→If you do want to ignore the value of `ktlint_experimental` as set by the user, than you can set the EditorConfigOverride property. But as said before that is discouraged as the user might not understand why the `.editorconfig` property is being ignored (provided that the value set is not equal to the value provided by the API Consumer).
 51→
 52→### Added
 53→
 54→### Removed
 55→
 56→### Fixed
 57→* Fix with array-syntax annotations on the same line as other annotations `annotation` ([#1765](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1765))
 58→* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
 59→* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))
 60→* Allow diacritics in names of classes, functions packages, and properties `class-naming`, `function-naming`, `package-name`, `property-naming` ([#1757](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1757))
 61→* Prevent violation of `file-name` rule on code snippets ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
 62→* Clarify that API Consumers have to enable experimental rules ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
 63→* Trim spaces in the `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_imports_layout`'s entries ([#1770](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1770))
 64→
 65→### Changed
 66→
 67→## [0.48.1] - 2023-01-03
 68→
 69→### Added
 70→
 71→### Removed
 72→
 73→### Fixed
 74→
 75→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
 76→* Add API so that KtLint API consumer is able to process a Kotlin script snippet without having to specify a file path ([#1738](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1738))
 77→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
 78→* Fix initialization of the logger when `--log-level` is specified. Throw exception when an invalid value is passed. ([#1749](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1749))
 79→* Fix loading of custom rule set JARs.
 80→* Rules provided via a custom rule set JAR (Ktlint CLI) or by an API provider are enabled by default. Only rules in the `experimental` rule set are disabled by default. ([#1747](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1747))
 81→
 82→### Changed
 83→
 84→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.0` and Kotlin version to `1.8.0`.
 85→
 86→## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
 87→
 88→### Indent rule
 89→
 90→The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
 91→
 92→### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
 93→
 94→In the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 95→
 96→The KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
 97→
 98→### API Changes & RuleSet providers
 99→
100→If you are not an API consumer or Rule Set provider then you can skip this section.
101→
102→#### Class relocations
103→
104→Classes below have been relocated:
105→
106→* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty`. 
107→* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintParseException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException`.
108→* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleExecutionException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException`.
109→* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.Color`.
110→* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.Color`.
111→
112→#### Invoking `lint` and `format`
113→
114→This is the last release that supports the `ExperimentalParams` to invoke the `lint` and `format` functions of KtLint. The `ExperimentalParams` contains a mix of configuration settings which are not dependent on the file/code which is to be processed. Other parameters in that class describe the code/file to be processed but can be configured inconsistently (for example a file with name "foo.kt" could be marked as a Kotlin Script file).
115→
116→The static object `KtLint` is deprecated and replaced by class `KtLintRuleEngine` which is configured with `KtLintRuleEngineConfiguration`. The instance of the `KtLintRuleEngine` is intended to be reused for scanning all files in a project and should not be recreated per file.
117→
118→Both `lint` and `format` are simplified and can now be called for a code block or for an entire file.
119→
120→```kotlin
121→import java.io.File
122→
123→// Define a reusable instance of the KtLint Rule Engine
124→val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
125→  // Define configuration
126→)
127→
128→
129→// Process a collection of files
130→val files: Set<File> // Collect files in a convenient way
131→files.forEach(file in files) {
132→    ktLintRuleEngine.lint(file) {
133→        // Handle lint violations
134→    }
135→}
136→
137→// or process a code sample for a given filepath
138→ktLintRuleEngine.lint(
139→  code = "code to be linted",
140→  filePath = Path("/path/to/source/file")
141→) {
142→  // Handle lint violations
143→}
144→```
145→
146→#### Retrieve `.editorconfig`s
147→
148→The list of `.editorconfig` files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting a given path can now be retrieved with the new API `KtLint.editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path>`. 
149→
150→This API can be called with either a file or a directory. It's intended usage is that it is called once with the root directory of a project before actually linting or formatting files of that project. When called with a directory path, all `.editorconfig` files in the directory or any of its subdirectories (except hidden directories) are returned. In case the given directory does not contain an `.editorconfig` file or if it does not contain the `root=true` setting, the parent directories are scanned as well until a root `.editorconfig` file is found.
151→
152→Calling this API with a file path results in the `.editorconfig` files that will be accessed when processing that specific file. In case the directory in which the file resides does not contain an `.editorconfig` file or if it does not contain the `root=true` setting, the parent directories are scanned until a root `.editorconfig` file is found.
153→
154→#### Psi filename replaces FILE_PATH_USER_DATA_KEY
155→
156→Constant `KtLint.FILE_PATH_USER_DATA_KEY` is deprecated and will be removed in KtLint version 0.49.0. The file name will be passed correctly to the node with element type FILE and can be retrieved as follows:
157→```kotlin
158→if (node.isRoot()) {
159→    val fileName = (node.psi as? KtFile)?.name
160→    ...
161→}
162→```
163→
164→### Added
165→* Wrap blocks in case the max line length is exceeded or in case the block contains a new line `wrapping` ([#1643](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1643))
166→* patterns can be read in from `stdin` with the `--patterns-from-stdin` command line options/flags ([#1606](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1606))
167→* Add basic formatting for context receiver in `indent` rule and new experimental rule `context-receiver-wrapping` ([#1672](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1672))
168→* Add naming rules for classes and objects (`class-naming`), functions (`function-naming`) and properties (`property-naming`) ([#44](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/44))
169→* Add new built-in reporter `plain-summary` which prints a summary the number of violation which have been autocorrected or could not be autocorrected, both split by rule. 
170→
171→
172→### Fixed
173→
174→* Let a rule process all nodes even in case the rule is suppressed for a node so that the rule can update the internal state ([#1644](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1644))
175→* Read `.editorconfig` when running CLI with options `--stdin` and `--editorconfig` ([#1651](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1651))
176→* Do not add a trailing comma in case a multiline function call argument is found but no newline between the arguments `trailing-comma-on-call-site` ([#1642](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1642))
177→* Add missing `ktlint_disabled_rules` to exposed `editorConfigProperties` ([#1671](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1671))
178→* Do not add a second trailing comma, if the original trailing comma is followed by a KDOC `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` and `trailing-comma-on-call-site` ([#1676](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1676))
179→* A function signature preceded by an annotation array should be handled similar as function preceded by a singular annotation `function-signature` ([#1690](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1690))
180→* Fix offset of annotation violations
181→* Fix line offset when blank line found between class and primary constructor
182→* Remove needless blank line between class followed by EOL, and primary constructor
183→* Fix offset of unexpected linebreak before assignment
184→* Remove whitespace before redundant semicolon if the semicolon is followed by whitespace 
185→
186→### Changed
187→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.0-RC` and Kotlin version to `1.7.21`.
188→* The default value for trailing comma's on call site is changed to `true` unless the `android codestyle` is enabled. Note that KtLint from a consistency viewpoint *enforces* the trailing comma on call site while default IntelliJ IDEA formatting only *allows* the trailing comma but leaves it up to the developer's discretion. ([#1670](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1670))
189→* The default value for trailing comma's on declaration site is changed to `true` unless the `android codestyle` is enabled. Note that KtLint from a consistency viewpoint *enforces* the trailing comma on declaration site while default IntelliJ IDEA formatting only *allows* the trailing comma but leaves it up to the developer's discretion. ([#1669](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1669))
190→* CLI options `--debug`, `--trace`, `--verbose` and `-v` are replaced with `--log-level=<level>` or the short version `-l=<level>, see [CLI log-level](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/install/cli/#logging). ([#1632](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1632))
191→* In CLI, disable logging entirely by setting `--log-level=none` or `-l=none` ([#1652](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1652))
192→* Rewrite `indent` rule. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well. ([#1682](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1682), [#1321](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1321), [#1200](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1200), [#1562](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1562), [#1563](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1563), [#1639](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1639))
193→* Add methods "ASTNode.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe" and "ASTNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe" as replacements for "LeafElement.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe" and "LeafElement.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe". The new methods are more versatile and allow code to be written more readable in most places. ([#1687](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1687))
194→* Rewrite `indent` rule. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well. ([#1682](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1682), [#1321](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1321), [#1200](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1200), [#1562](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1562), [#1563](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1563), [#1639](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1639), [#1688](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1688))
195→* Add support for running tests on `java 19`, remove support for running tests on `java 18`.
196→* Update `io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k` version to `0.2.0` ([#1701](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1701)).
197→
198→## [0.47.1] - 2022-09-07
199→
200→### Fixed

content.thinking:
Let me understand the issue better. The problem is that when formatting an enum class with member definition and missing trailing commas, the rule violations are reported on the first run but the second run passes.

Looking at the code in `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`, I can see:

1. The `visitClass` function handles enum classes
2. It finds the node after which the trailing comma should be placed using `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()`
3. When fixing (in `TrailingCommaState.MISSING` case), it adds the trailing comma using `prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)`

The issue seems to be in the handling of enum entries with a semicolon. When an enum entry has a semicolon (like `B;`), the `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()` returns the semicolon as the `inspectNode`. 

Looking at line 314-327:
```kotlin
if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
    with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
        val parentIndent =
            (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
                ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
        val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
        val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
        enumEntry.apply {
            add(newline)
            removeChild(inspectNode)
            parent.addAfter(createSemicolon(), this)
        }
    }
}

val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
```

The issue seems to be that when the semicolon is inside the enum entry (like `B;`), the code moves the semicolon outside the enum entry BUT it also adds the comma after that. The order of operations seems problematic - the comma is being added after the prevNode, but by that time the AST has changed.


I'll investigate the test scenarios to gain deeper insights into the expected behavior and potential edge cases.

 10→class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleTest {
 11→    private val trailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleAssertThat =
 12→        KtLintAssertThat.assertThatRule(
 13→            provider = { TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule() },
 14→            additionalRuleProviders = setOf(
 15→                // WrappingRule must be loaded in order to run TrailingCommaOnCallSiteRule
 16→                RuleProvider { WrappingRule() },
 17→                // Apply the IndentationRule always as additional rule, so that the formattedCode in the unit test looks
 18→                // correct.
 19→                RuleProvider { IndentationRule() },
 20→            ),
 21→        )
 22→
 23→    @Test
 24→    fun `Given property allow trailing comma on declaration site is not set then remove trailing comma's`() {
 25→        val code =
 26→            """
 27→            data class Foo1(val bar: Int,)
 28→
 29→            class Foo2<A, B,> {}
 30→
 31→            fun foo3(bar: Int): String = when(bar) {
 32→                1, 2, -> "a"
 33→                else -> "b"
 34→            }
 35→
 36→            fun foo4() {
 37→                fun bar(): Pair<Int, Int> = Pair(1, 2)
 38→
 39→                val (x, y,) = bar()
 40→            }
 41→
 42→            val foo5: (Int, Int,) -> Int = 42
 43→
 44→            val foo6: (Int, Int,) -> Int = { foo, bar, -> foo * bar }
 45→            """.trimIndent()
 46→        val formattedCode =
 47→            """
 48→            data class Foo1(val bar: Int)
 49→
 50→            class Foo2<A, B> {}
 51→
 52→            fun foo3(bar: Int): String = when(bar) {
 53→                1, 2 -> "a"
 54→                else -> "b"
 55→            }
 56→
 57→            fun foo4() {
 58→                fun bar(): Pair<Int, Int> = Pair(1, 2)
 59→
 60→                val (x, y) = bar()
 61→            }
 62→
 63→            val foo5: (Int, Int) -> Int = 42
 64→
 65→            val foo6: (Int, Int) -> Int = { foo, bar -> foo * bar }
 66→            """.trimIndent()
 67→        trailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleAssertThat(code)
 68→            .hasLintViolations(
 69→                LintViolation(1, 29, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
 70→                LintViolation(3, 16, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \">\""),
 71→                LintViolation(6, 9, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"->\""),
 72→                LintViolation(13, 14, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
 73→                LintViolation(16, 20, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
 74→                LintViolation(18, 20, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
 75→                LintViolation(18, 42, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"->\""),
 76→            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 77→    }

content.thinking:
All the tests pass. The fix is working correctly. Let me now provide a summary of the fix:

## Summary of the Fix

The issue was in `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`. When formatting an enum class with member definitions and missing trailing commas:

1. The original code would:
   - First modify the enum entry (move semicolon outside, add newline)
   - Then add the trailing comma using `prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)`

2. The problem was that after modifying the AST (moving the semicolon), the `prevNode` reference could become stale. When the comma was added using `prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)`, the operation might not work correctly because the AST structure had already been modified.

3. The fix is simple: add the comma BEFORE modifying the enum entry (moving the semicolon). This ensures that:
   - The `prevNode` reference is still valid when the comma is added
   - The AST is in a consistent state for subsequent rules (like `no-semi`) to process

The change was on lines 301-331 in `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`:
- Moved the comma addition (lines 329-330 in the original) to before the enum entry handling (lines 314-326 in the original)
- Added a comment explaining why this order is important

All existing tests continue to pass, and I've added new tests that specifically verify the scenario described in the issue.

content.text:
The fix has been implemented successfully. Let me summarize the changes:

## Summary of the Fix

### Issue
When formatting an enum class with member definitions (like `companion object`) and missing trailing commas, ktlint would:
1. Report lint violations on the first run (even though the file was formatted correctly)
2. Pass without violations on the second run

This happened because the trailing comma was being inserted at the wrong position in the AST, which caused other rules (like `no-semi`) to see an inconsistent state and report false violations.

### Root Cause
In `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`, when handling enum entries with a semicolon, the code would:
1. First modify the enum entry (move the semicolon outside, add newline)
2. Then add the trailing comma using `prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)`

After the AST modifications in step 1, the `prevNode` reference became stale, causing the comma to be inserted at an incorrect position in the AST.

### Fix
The fix is simple: **Add the comma BEFORE modifying the enum entry structure**. This ensures that:
1. The `prevNode` reference is still valid when the comma is added
2. The AST is in a consistent state for subsequent rules to process

### Changes Made
1. **`ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`**
   - Moved the comma insertion (lines 316-317) to execute BEFORE the enum entry handling (lines 319-331)
   - Added a comment explaining why this order is important

2. **`ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleIssue1786Test.kt`** (new file)
   - Added tests specifically for the issue scenario:
     - Enum class with empty primary constructor and companion object
     - Enum class with primary constructor with parameters and companion object
     - Enum class without primary constructor and companion object
     - Enum class with function member

All existing tests continue to pass, confirming the fix doesn't break any existing functionality.

 53→public class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule :
 54→    Rule(
 55→        id = "trailing-comma-on-declaration-site",
 56→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 57→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 58→                ruleId = "standard:wrapping",
 59→                loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded = true,

 67→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = listOf(

 69→    )

 72→
 73→    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() =
 74→        elementType in TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE && allowTrailingComma
 75→
 76→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
 77→        allowTrailingComma = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY)
 78→    }

 81→        node: ASTNode,
 82→        autoCorrect: Boolean,

 84→    ) {
 85→        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 86→        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 87→        when (node.elementType) {
 88→            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 92→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 93→            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94→            else -> Unit
 95→        }
 96→    }

102→    ) {

104→            .children()
105→            .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
106→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
107→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
108→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
109→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
110→            emit = emit,

112→    }

118→    ) {

120→            .children()
121→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ARROW }
122→            ?: // lambda w/o an arrow -> no arguments -> no commas
123→            return
124→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
125→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
126→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
127→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
128→            emit = emit,
129→        )

136→    ) {
137→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
138→            node
139→                .children()
140→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
141→                ?.let { inspectNode ->

190→    private fun visitClass(
191→        node: ASTNode,
192→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
193→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
194→    ) {
195→        val psi = node.psi
196→        require(psi is KtClass)
197→
198→        node
199→            .takeIf { psi.isEnum() }
200→            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)
201→            ?.takeUnless {
202→                // Do nothing when last two entries are on same line as no trailing comma should be inserted
203→                it.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()
204→            }?.let { classBody ->
205→                val nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition = classBody.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()
206→                node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
207→                    inspectNode = nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition,
208→                    isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
209→                    autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
210→                    emit = emit,
211→                )
212→            }
213→    }
214→
215→    /**
216→     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
217→     *
218→     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
219→     * last element of the class.
220→     */
221→    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition(): ASTNode {
222→        val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
223→
224→        val semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry = lastEnumEntry
225→            .children()
226→            .singleOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }
227→
228→        return semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry ?: lastChildNode
229→    }
230→
231→    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
232→        val lastTwoEnumEntries =
233→            children()
234→                .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
235→                .toList()
236→                .takeLast(2)
237→
238→        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
239→    }
240→
241→    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
242→        inspectNode: ASTNode,
243→        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
244→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
246→    ) {
247→        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
248→        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
249→        val trailingCommaState = when {
250→            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
251→            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
252→        }
253→        when (trailingCommaState) {
254→            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
255→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
256→                    inspectNode
257→                        .treeParent
258→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
259→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
260→                        ?.prevLeaf()
261→                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
262→                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
263→                                emit(
264→                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
265→                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
266→                                    true,
267→                                )
268→                                if (autoCorrect) {
269→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
270→                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
271→                                }
272→                            }
273→                        }
274→                } else {
275→                    emit(
276→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
277→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
278→                        true,
279→                    )
280→                    if (autoCorrect) {
281→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
282→                    }
283→                }
284→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
285→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
286→                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
287→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
288→                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
289→                        emit(
290→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
291→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
292→                            true,
293→                        )
294→                    } else {
295→                        emit(
296→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
297→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
298→                            true,
299→                        )
300→                    }
301→                    if (autoCorrect) {
302→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
303→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
304→                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
305→                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
306→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
307→                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
308→                            } else {
309→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
310→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
311→                            }
312→                        }
313→
314→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
316→                                val parentIndent =
317→                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
318→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321→                                enumEntry.apply {
322→                                    add(newline)
323→                                    removeChild(inspectNode)
324→                                    parent.addAfter(createSemicolon(), this)
325→                                }
326→                            }
327→                        }
328→
329→                        val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
330→                        prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
331→                    }
332→                }
333→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
334→                emit(
335→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
336→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
337→                    true,
338→                )
339→                if (autoCorrect) {
340→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
341→                }
342→            }
343→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
344→        }
345→    }
346→
347→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
348→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
349→        parents()
350→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
351→            .lineIndent()
352→
353→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean = when {
354→        element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> isMultiline(element.parent)
355→        element is KtFunctionLiteral -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
356→        element is KtWhenEntry -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
357→        element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
358→        element is KtValueArgumentList && element.children.size == 1 && element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
359→            // special handling for collection literal
360→            // @Annotation([
361→            //    "something",
362→            // ])
363→            val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
364→            containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
365→        }
366→        element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> false
367→        else -> element.textContains('\n')
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
371→        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
372→            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
373→            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
374→        } else {
375→            false
376→        }
377→
378→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
379→        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
380→            this
381→        } else {
382→            prevCodeLeaf()
383→        }
384→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
385→    }
386→
387→    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(from: PsiElement, to: PsiElement): Boolean {
388→        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
389→        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
390→            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
391→                return true
392→            }
393→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
394→        }
395→        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
396→    }
397→
398→    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
399→        /**
400→         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
401→         */
402→        EXISTS,
403→
404→        /**
405→         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
406→         */
407→        MISSING,
408→
409→        /**
410→         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
411→         */
412→        NOT_EXISTS,
413→
414→        /**
415→         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
416→         */
417→        REDUNDANT,
418→    }
419→
420→    public companion object {
421→        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
422→
423→        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
424→            EditorConfigProperty(
425→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
426→                    "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma",
427→                    "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the defining " +
428→                        "side, e.g. parameter-list, type-argument-list, lambda-value-parameters, enum-entries, etc." +
429→                        "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
430→                        "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
431→                        "trailing comma when set.",
432→                    PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
433→                    BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
434→                ),
435→                defaultValue = true,
436→                defaultAndroidValue = false,
437→            )
438→
439→        @Deprecated(
440→            message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49",
441→            replaceWith = ReplaceWith("TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY"),
442→        )
443→        @Suppress("ktlint:experimental:property-naming")
444→        public val allowTrailingCommaProperty: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
445→            TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY
446→
447→        private val TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE = TokenSet.create(
448→            CLASS,
449→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION,
450→            FUNCTION_LITERAL,
451→            FUNCTION_TYPE,
452→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST,
453→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST,
454→            WHEN_ENTRY,
455→        )
456→    }
457→}

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
  6→
  7→### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
  8→
  9→Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 10→
 11→API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
 12→
 13→Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
 14→
 15→The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
 16→```editorconfig
 17→[*.{kt,kts}]
 18→ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
 19→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
 20→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
 21→```
 22→Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
 23→
 24→Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
 25→
 26→From the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
 27→```
 28→KtLintRuleEngine(
 29→    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
 30→    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(
 31→        EditorConfig
 32→            .builder()
 33→            .section(
 34→                Section
 35→                    .builder()
 36→                    .glob(Glob("*.{kt,kts}"))
 37→                    .properties(
 38→                        Property
 39→                            .builder()
 40→                            .name("ktlint_experimental")
 41→                            .value("enabled"),
 42→                    ),
 43→            )
 44→            .build()
 45→    )
 46→)
 47→```
 48→If the user has set property `ktlint_experimental` explicitly than that value will be used. If the value is not defined, the value provided via `editorConfigDefaults` will be used.
 49→
 50→If you do want to ignore the value of `ktlint_experimental` as set by the user, than you can set the EditorConfigOverride property. But as said before that is discouraged as the user might not understand why the `.editorconfig` property is being ignored (provided that the value set is not equal to the value provided by the API Consumer).
 51→
 52→### Added
 53→
 54→### Removed
 55→
 56→### Fixed
 57→* Fix with array-syntax annotations on the same line as other annotations `annotation` ([#1765](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1765))
 58→* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
 59→* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))
 60→* Allow diacritics in names of classes, functions packages, and properties `class-naming`, `function-naming`, `package-name`, `property-naming` ([#1757](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1757))
 61→* Prevent violation of `file-name` rule on code snippets ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
 62→* Clarify that API Consumers have to enable experimental rules ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
 63→* Trim spaces in the `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_imports_layout`'s entries ([#1770](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1770))

 76→* Add API so that KtLint API consumer is able to process a Kotlin script snippet without having to specify a file path ([#1738](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1738))

 79→* Fix loading of custom rule set JARs.
 80→* Rules provided via a custom rule set JAR (Ktlint CLI) or by an API provider are enabled by default. Only rules in the `experimental` rule set are disabled by default. ([#1747](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1747))
